name: Airtel Money Africa Mobile Money API
what_it_does: Airtel Money REST API for businesses in Sub-Saharan Africa to accept and disburse mobile money payments through Airtel's mobile money network across 14 African countries including Uganda, Zambia, Kenya, Tanzania, Rwanda, Congo, Nigeria, and others, enabling merchant collection (C2B), disbursements (B2C), account balance inquiry, and transaction status for Airtel's 34M+ mobile money users across Africa. Enables AI agents to manage merchant collection for Africa mobile money payment automation, handle disbursement (B2C) for Africa payout and salary automation, access airtime top-up for Africa mobile credit automation, retrieve transaction status for Africa payment confirmation automation, manage account balance inquiry for Africa wallet balance automation, handle KYC verification for Africa identity check automation, access cross-border transfer for Africa mobile money remittance automation, retrieve transaction history for Africa payment analytics automation, manage webhook callback for Africa payment event automation, and integrate Airtel Money with African e-commerce, NGO, fintech, and enterprise platforms for end-to-end Sub-Saharan Africa mobile money automation.
use_cases: Collecting mobile money payments for Africa e-commerce and services checkout agents
Disbursing salaries and benefits to Africa mobile wallets from payroll agents
Topping up airtime for Africa mobile credit agents
Confirming Airtel Money transactions from Africa fulfillment agents
not_for: Non-Africa markets (Airtel Money API is Sub-Saharan Africa only)
M-Pesa Kenya users (Airtel Money serves Airtel subscribers; M-Pesa serves Safaricom)
Large international bank transfers (Airtel Money is mobile wallet for sub-$500 transactions)
best_when: An African business, NGO, or fintech wanting AI agents to automate mobile money collection and disbursement across Airtel's 14-country Sub-Saharan Africa network for markets where Airtel is the dominant mobile operator.
avoid_when: COUNTRY-SPECIFIC API ENDPOINTS: Airtel Money uses country-specific API endpoints and credentials; automated multi-country deployment must configure per-country endpoints and credentials; automated single-endpoint assumption for all Airtel Africa countries creates wrong_country_routing. ASYNC CALLBACK FOR ALL OPERATIONS: Airtel Money collection and disbursement results are delivered via callback URL; automated workflow must use callback not synchronous polling for result; automated immediate synchronous result expectation creates timeout awaiting callback. MSISDN MUST BE AIRTEL SUBSCRIBER: Airtel Money requires recipient/payer to be active Airtel subscriber with mobile money account; automated payment must verify subscriber status; automated payment to non-Airtel or inactive wallet creates subscriber_not_found or wallet_not_active. SANDBOX IS SIMULATED — NOT REAL WALLETS: Airtel Money sandbox uses simulated responses not real wallet transactions; automated sandbox testing must use Airtel-provided test MSISDNs; automated real Airtel number in sandbox creates unexpected behavior.

auth.notes: Airtel Money uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials for API authentication. REST API with JSON. London, UK HQ (Airtel Africa plc). NYSE/LSE: AAF. Products: C2B collection, B2C disbursement, airtime top-up, balance inquiry, transaction status, KYC. No public SDK. Regulated in 14 African countries. 34M+ mobile money users. Countries: Uganda, Zambia, Kenya, Tanzania, Rwanda, Nigeria, DRC, Malawi, Madagascar, Niger, Chad, Congo Brazzaville, Gabon, Seychelles.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox free; production requires partnership with Airtel in each country
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-transaction fees negotiated per country; typically 0.5-2% per collection/disbursement in local currency
pricing.notes: London UK/Africa. NYSE:AAF. Per-transaction pricing in local currency per country. Regulated in each country.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth app per country; collection and disbursement APIs throttled; callback-based async

known_agent_gotchas: COUNTRY-SPECIFIC BASE URL AND CREDENTIALS: Airtel Money has separate API base URLs and OAuth credentials per country (UG, ZM, KE, TZ differ); automated multi-country must configure routing per country; automated single-endpoint multi-country creates wrong_country or authentication failure

CALLBACK URL IS REQUIRED AND MUST BE PUBLIC HTTPS: Airtel Money callback URL must be registered and publicly accessible HTTPS; automated setup must provide valid HTTPS callback; automated localhost or HTTP callback creates callback_delivery_failure and missed payment results

RESULT DELIVERED VIA CALLBACK NOT RESPONSE: Airtel Money collection and B2C responses return transaction ID only; actual result (success/failure) delivered to callback URL; automated immediate status check after initiation creates pending status not final result

AIRTEL SUBSCRIBER VALIDATION BEFORE COLLECTION: Airtel Money payer must be active Airtel subscriber; automated collection should verify subscriber before initiating; automated collection to invalid/non-Airtel MSISDN creates subscriber_not_found after full round-trip delay

TOKEN EXPIRY PER COUNTRY: Airtel Money OAuth token validity may differ per country deployment; automated integration must handle per-country token expiry; automated shared token across countries creates cross-country auth failure
agent_readiness.security_notes: Africa mobile money. Country-specific telecom regulation. OAuth2. Sub-Saharan Africa. Mobile wallet data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Africa mobile money. Telecom-regulated per country. OAuth2. Sub-Saharan Africa. Mobile wallet data.
